_INSTRUCTIONS.purpose: SIMPLIFIED SUBSET of AEM Forms grammar for AI-powered rule generation. This is a SUBSET of annotated_grammar.json - all structures are AEM-compatible.

node_types.SEQUENCE: Contains items in exact order. Must include all items specified in the rule.
node_types.CHOICE: Select exactly ONE option based on natural language keywords.
node_types.ARRAY: Contains one or more items of the same type (e.g., multiple actions).
node_types.TERMINAL: Leaf node containing actual values (form objects, literals, functions).
node_types.LITERAL_TOKEN: Words like 'When', 'Then', 'Else', 'to' that appear in rules as structure. Include as nodes with nodeName set to the literal word and value: null.

critical_rules.VARIABLE_transparency: When a node has rule: 'VARIABLE', do NOT create a VARIABLE node. Instead, put the actual value directly in the parent node.
critical_rules.CONDITION_property: All CONDITION nodes MUST include property 'nested': false
critical_rules.trigger_vs_condition: Trigger event (click, change) is separate from conditions (field equals value). Never combine them with AND/OR.
critical_rules.else_optional: Only include Else block and second BLOCK_STATEMENTS when else actions are explicitly mentioned in natural language.
critical_rules.no_arithmetic_expressions: For calculations like a + b * c, use FUNCTION_CALL with a custom function instead of BINARY_EXPRESSION.
critical_rules.function_for_complexity: Any complex logic (string concat, API calls, data transformation) should be done via FUNCTION_CALL with custom functions.

not_supported.alternative: Use FUNCTION_CALL with custom functions for complex logic

_PATTERN_TEMPLATE.description: Standard structure for all trigger event rules

items: TRIGGER_EVENT (standard: 'is clicked', 'is changed', 'is initialised' OR custom event name)
TRIGGER_EVENT_SCRIPTS (contains condition, then, actions, else, else-actions)

ROOT.description: The root node of any rule. Always start here. Contains exactly ONE item: a STATEMENT node.

STATEMENT.description: Top-level statement type. For trigger event rules, always choose TRIGGER_SCRIPTS.
STATEMENT.how_to_choose: For When-Then-Else rules, always select TRIGGER_SCRIPTS

TRIGGER_SCRIPTS.description: Container for a single trigger-based rule. Contains exactly ONE item: SINGLE_TRIGGER_SCRIPTS.

SINGLE_TRIGGER_SCRIPTS.description: SEQUENCE NODE with exactly 4 items in order: 1) COMPONENT (what triggers), 2) TRIGGER_EVENT (event type), 3) 'When' literal token, 4) TRIGGER_EVENT_SCRIPTS (condition + actions).
items_order: 1. COMPONENT - The form component that triggers the rule
2. TRIGGER_EVENT - The event type (standard: 'is clicked', 'is changed', 'is initialised' OR custom event)

COMPONENT.description: TERMINAL NODE: A form component (button, text field, etc.). Use VARIABLE transparency rule - put form object directly here, do NOT create VARIABLE node.
COMPONENT.type_reference: Use full type from treeJson: FORM (for form), AFCOMPONENT|PANEL (for panels), AFCOMPONENT|FIELD|TEXT FIELD|STRING (for text inputs), etc.

TRIGGER_EVENT.description: TERMINAL NODE: The event that triggers the rule. Standard events: 'is clicked', 'is changed', 'is initialised'. Custom events: use exact name provided by user.

custom_events.description: If event doesn't match standard events, use exact name provided by user

TRIGGER_EVENT_SCRIPTS.description: SEQUENCE NODE with 5 items: 1) CONDITION (optional), 2) 'Then' literal token, 3) BLOCK_STATEMENTS (actions), 4) 'Else' literal token (only if else actions exist), 5) BLOCK_STATEMENTS for else (only if else actions exist).
items_order: 1. CONDITION - Optional condition to check (can be empty/always true)
3. BLOCK_STATEMENTS - Actions to execute when condition is true
4. 'Else' literal token - {nodeName: 'Else', value: null} - ONLY if else actions mentioned
5. BLOCK_STATEMENTS - Actions for else - ONLY if else actions mentioned

CONDITION.description: CHOICE NODE: Select COMPARISON_EXPRESSION for simple single comparisons. Select BOOLEAN_BINARY_EXPRESSION when condition contains AND/OR logic. MUST include 'nested: false' property. For EMPTY/NO condition, use choice: null (NOT {} empty object!).

how_to_choose.BOOLEAN_BINARY_EXPRESSION: Multiple conditions with AND/OR: 'field equals 5 AND textinput is not empty'
how_to_choose.null: NO condition (always execute) - use choice: null

CONDITION.empty_condition: When there is NO condition (always execute), use choice: null - NOT {} empty object!

structure_empty._note: Use this when there is NO condition - choice is null, NOT {}

COMPARISON_EXPRESSION.description: SEQUENCE NODE: Single comparison with 3 items: 1) left EXPRESSION, 2) OPERATOR, 3) right EXPRESSION.
items_order: 1. EXPRESSION - Left side (usually a form component)
2. OPERATOR - Comparison operator (equals, greater than, is empty, etc.)
3. EXPRESSION - Right side (value, literal, or another component) - NOTE: For unary operators like IS_EMPTY, this may be omitted

BOOLEAN_BINARY_EXPRESSION.description: SEQUENCE NODE: Combines two conditions with AND/OR. Contains 3 items: 1) left CONDITION, 2) OPERATOR (AND/OR), 3) right CONDITION.

EXPRESSION.rule: COMPONENT | STRING_LITERAL | NUMERIC_LITERAL | BOOLEAN_LITERAL | DATE_LITERAL | FUNCTION_CALL | UTM_PARAMETER | QUERY_PARAMETER | BROWSER_DETAILS | URL_DETAILS | GET_VARIABLE
EXPRESSION.description: CHOICE NODE: Select based on value type and source. NOTE: For complex operations (arithmetic, string concat), use FUNCTION_CALL with a custom function.

how_to_choose.STRING_LITERAL: Text values in quotes or mentioned as text (e.g., 'hello', "test")
how_to_choose.FUNCTION_CALL: Function names or any calculated/complex values (e.g., 'calculateTotal()', 'formatName(first, last)')
how_to_choose.UTM_PARAMETER: UTM tracking parameters (utm_source, utm_medium, utm_campaign, utm_term, utm_content)

OPERATOR.rule: LESS_THAN | GREATER_THAN | EQUALS_TO | NOT_EQUALS_TO | CONTAINS | DOES_NOT_CONTAIN | STARTS_WITH | ENDS_WITH | AND | OR | HAS_SELECTED | IS_NOT_EMPTY | IS_EMPTY | IS_TRUE | IS_FALSE | IS_BEFORE | IS_AFTER | IS_VALID | IS_NOT_VALID
OPERATOR.description: CHOICE NODE: Select operator based on natural language. NOTE: Arithmetic operators (PLUS, MINUS, MULTIPLY, DIVIDE) are NOT in this subset - use FUNCTION_CALL for calculations.

EQUALS_TO.description: Equality comparison. Use when NL says 'equals', 'is equal to', 'is', 'same as'.

NOT_EQUALS_TO.description: Not equal comparison. Use when NL says 'not equals', 'is not equal to', '!=', 'different from'.

GREATER_THAN.description: Greater than comparison. Use when NL says 'greater than', 'more than', '>'.

LESS_THAN.description: Less than comparison. Use when NL says 'less than', 'smaller than', '<'.

CONTAINS.description: Contains check. Use when NL says 'contains', 'includes'.

DOES_NOT_CONTAIN.description: Does not contain. Use when NL says 'does not contain', 'excludes'.

STARTS_WITH.description: String starts with. Use when NL says 'starts with', 'begins with'.

ENDS_WITH.description: String ends with. Use when NL says 'ends with', 'finishes with'.

AND.description: Logical AND. Both conditions must be true. Use when NL says 'and', '&&'.

OR.description: Logical OR. At least one condition must be true. Use when NL says 'or', '||'.

IS_EMPTY.description: Checks if field is empty/blank. Unary operator (no right expression needed). Use when NL says 'is empty', 'is blank', 'has no value'.

IS_NOT_EMPTY.description: Checks if field has value. Unary operator. Use when NL says 'is not empty', 'has value', 'is filled'.

IS_TRUE.description: Checks if boolean is true. Unary operator. Use when NL says 'is true', 'is checked'.

IS_FALSE.description: Checks if boolean is false. Unary operator. Use when NL says 'is false', 'is unchecked'.

IS_BEFORE.description: Date comparison - checks if date is before another. Use when NL says 'is before', 'before'.

IS_AFTER.description: Date comparison - checks if date is after another. Use when NL says 'is after', 'after'.

HAS_SELECTED.description: Checks if multi-select field has a specific value selected. Use when NL says 'has selected', 'selected'.

IS_VALID.description: Checks if field passes validation. Unary operator. Use when NL says 'is valid', 'valid'.

IS_NOT_VALID.description: Checks if field fails validation. Unary operator. Use when NL says 'is not valid', 'invalid'.

UTM_PARAMETER.description: TERMINAL NODE: Access UTM tracking parameters from URL. Options: utm_source, utm_medium, utm_campaign, utm_term, utm_content.

QUERY_PARAMETER.description: TERMINAL NODE: Access URL query parameters. Use when NL mentions 'query param', 'URL parameter', or specific param names.

BROWSER_DETAILS.description: TERMINAL NODE: Access browser information. Options: userAgent, language, platform.

URL_DETAILS.description: TERMINAL NODE: Access URL components. Options: hostname, pathname.

GET_VARIABLE.description: SEQUENCE NODE: Retrieve a stored variable value. Contains 4 items: 1) 'key' literal, 2) VARIABLE_NAME, 3) 'from' literal, 4) AFCOMPONENT.
items_order: 2. VARIABLE_NAME - The variable key (string or component value)
4. AFCOMPONENT - The component storing the variable

VARIABLE_NAME.description: CHOICE NODE: The variable key - can be a string, component value, function result, or another variable.

how_to_choose.AFCOMPONENT: Variable name from a form field

VARIABLE_VALUE.rule: STRING_LITERAL | NUMERIC_LITERAL | BOOLEAN_LITERAL | AFCOMPONENT | FUNCTION_CALL | GET_VARIABLE
VARIABLE_VALUE.description: CHOICE NODE: The value to store in a variable.

MEMBER_EXPRESSION.description: SEQUENCE NODE: Access a property of a component. Contains 3 items: 1) PROPERTY_LIST, 2) 'of' literal, 3) COMPONENT. Used in SET_PROPERTY action.
items_order: 3. COMPONENT - The component whose property to access

PROPERTY_LIST.description: TERMINAL NODE: A property name to access from a component. Common properties: visible, enabled, label, placeholder, required, value, readOnly.

PANEL.description: TERMINAL NODE: A panel component. Use VARIABLE transparency - put panel object directly.

BLOCK_STATEMENTS.description: ARRAY NODE: Contains one or more actions to execute. Each action mentioned in NL becomes a separate BLOCK_STATEMENT item.

BLOCK_STATEMENT.rule: HIDE_STATEMENT | SHOW_STATEMENT | SET_VALUE_STATEMENT | FUNCTION_CALL | ENABLE_STATEMENT | DISABLE_STATEMENT | CLEAR_VALUE_STATEMENT | SUBMIT_FORM | RESET_FORM | VALIDATE_FORM | SAVE_FORM | NAVIGATE_TO | DISPATCH_EVENT | SET_FOCUS | ADD_INSTANCE | REMOVE_INSTANCE | NAVIGATE_IN_PANEL | SET_VARIABLE | SET_PROPERTY
BLOCK_STATEMENT.description: CHOICE NODE: Select action type based on NL keywords. NOTE: WSDL_STATEMENT and ASYNC_FUNCTION_CALL are NOT supported - use FUNCTION_CALL with custom functions instead.

how_to_choose.SET_VALUE_STATEMENT: set to, assign, make equal, set value
how_to_choose.FUNCTION_CALL: call function, invoke, execute function, API call, calculate, or function name
how_to_choose.VALIDATE_FORM: validate form, validate panel, validate field
how_to_choose.NAVIGATE_TO: navigate to, go to URL, open URL
how_to_choose.DISPATCH_EVENT: dispatch event, trigger event, fire event
how_to_choose.ADD_INSTANCE: add instance, add repeatable, add row
how_to_choose.REMOVE_INSTANCE: remove instance, remove repeatable, remove row
how_to_choose.NAVIGATE_IN_PANEL: navigate panel, go to next, go to previous, next panel, previous panel
how_to_choose.SET_PROPERTY: set property, change property, update label, change placeholder

HIDE_STATEMENT.description: Hides a component. Contains 1 item: AFCOMPONENT to hide. Use VARIABLE transparency - put form object directly.

SHOW_STATEMENT.description: Shows a component. Contains 1 item: AFCOMPONENT to show. Use VARIABLE transparency - put form object directly.

ENABLE_STATEMENT.description: Enables a component. Contains 1 item: AFCOMPONENT to enable.

DISABLE_STATEMENT.description: Disables a component. Contains 1 item: AFCOMPONENT to disable.

CLEAR_VALUE_STATEMENT.description: Clears value of a field. Contains 1 item: VALUE_FIELD to clear.

SUBMIT_FORM.description: Submits the form. No items needed - empty items array.

RESET_FORM.description: Resets form, panel, or component to initial state. Contains 1 item: component to reset. Use VARIABLE transparency.

VALIDATE_FORM.description: Validates form, panel, or component. Contains 1 item: component to validate. Use VARIABLE transparency.

SAVE_FORM.description: Saves the form data. No items needed - empty items array.

NAVIGATE_TO.description: Navigates to a URL. SEQUENCE with 3 items: 1) NAVIGATE_TO_EXPRESSION (URL), 2) 'in' literal, 3) NAVIGATE_METHOD_OPTIONS (window type).
items_order: 1. NAVIGATE_TO_EXPRESSION - URL to navigate to (URL_LITERAL, COMPONENT, or FUNCTION_CALL)

NAVIGATE_TO_EXPRESSION.description: CHOICE NODE: The URL to navigate to - can be literal URL, field value, or function result.

NAVIGATE_METHOD_OPTIONS.description: CHOICE NODE: How to open the URL.

NEW_WINDOW.description: Open URL in a new browser window.

NEW_TAB.description: Open URL in a new browser tab.

SAME_TAB.description: Open URL in the current browser tab.

URL_LITERAL.description: TERMINAL NODE: A literal URL string.

SET_FOCUS.description: Sets focus to a component. SEQUENCE with 2 items: 1) 'to' literal, 2) AFCOMPONENT.

ADD_INSTANCE.description: Adds instance to repeatable panel. SEQUENCE with 2 items: 1) 'of' literal, 2) REPEATABLE_COMPONENT.

REMOVE_INSTANCE.description: Removes instance from repeatable panel. SEQUENCE with 2 items: 1) 'of' literal, 2) REPEATABLE_COMPONENT.

REPEATABLE_COMPONENT.description: TERMINAL NODE: A repeatable panel component. Use VARIABLE transparency.

NAVIGATE_IN_PANEL.description: Navigates within a wizard/tabbed panel. SEQUENCE with 3 items: 1) PANEL_FOCUS_OPTION, 2) 'of' literal, 3) PANEL.
items_order: 3. PANEL - The panel to navigate within

PANEL_FOCUS_OPTION.description: CHOICE NODE: Direction to navigate in panel.

NEXT_ITEM.description: Navigate to the next item in the panel.

PREVIOUS_ITEM.description: Navigate to the previous item in the panel.

FIRST_ITEM.description: Navigate to the first item in the panel.

SET_VARIABLE.rule: key VARIABLE_NAME value VARIABLE_VALUE on AFCOMPONENT
SET_VARIABLE.description: Stores a variable value on a component. SEQUENCE with 6 items.
items_order: 2. VARIABLE_NAME - The variable key (string or expression)
6. AFCOMPONENT - The component to store the variable on

SET_VALUE_STATEMENT.description: Sets field value. SEQUENCE with 3 items: 1) VALUE_FIELD (target), 2) 'to' literal token, 3) EXPRESSION (new value).
items_order: 3. EXPRESSION - The value to set (use FUNCTION_CALL for calculated values)

FUNCTION_CALL.description: TERMINAL NODE: Calls a custom or OOTB function. CRITICAL: Uses 'functionName' (object) + 'params' (array), NOT 'value.args'.
FUNCTION_CALL.critical_structure_note: Structure is {nodeName, parentNodeName, functionName: {id,displayName,type,args,impl}, params: [EXPRESSION...]}. DO NOT use value.args pattern.

DISPATCH_EVENT.description: SEQUENCE NODE: Fires a custom event on a component. Contains 3 items: 1) STRING_LITERAL (event name), 2) 'on' literal token, 3) AFCOMPONENT (target component or form).
items_order: 1. STRING_LITERAL - The event name to dispatch
3. AFCOMPONENT - The target component or form to dispatch event on

SET_PROPERTY.description: SEQUENCE NODE: Sets a property of a component. Contains 3 items: 1) MEMBER_EXPRESSION (property of component), 2) 'to' literal token, 3) EXTENDED_EXPRESSION (new value).
items_order: 1. MEMBER_EXPRESSION - The property to set (e.g., 'visible of TextInput', 'label of Email')
3. EXTENDED_EXPRESSION - The value to set the property to

EXTENDED_EXPRESSION.rule: COMPONENT | DATE_LITERAL | STRING_LITERAL | BOOLEAN_LITERAL | NUMERIC_LITERAL | FUNCTION_CALL
EXTENDED_EXPRESSION.description: CHOICE NODE: Extended expression type used in SET_PROPERTY. Similar to EXPRESSION but used for property values.

STRING_LITERAL.description: TERMINAL NODE: A literal string value. Value is the string without quotes.

NUMERIC_LITERAL.description: TERMINAL NODE: A literal number value.

BOOLEAN_LITERAL.description: CHOICE NODE: Boolean value. Select True or False.

DATE_LITERAL.description: TERMINAL NODE: A date value in ISO format.

AFCOMPONENT.description: TERMINAL NODE: Any form component (used in actions like HIDE/SHOW). Use VARIABLE transparency - put form object directly here. Type should be 'AFCOMPONENT'.

VALUE_FIELD.description: TERMINAL NODE: A field that can have its value set. Use VARIABLE transparency - put form object directly. Use full type from treeJson.
VALUE_FIELD.type_reference: Use full type from treeJson: AFCOMPONENT|FIELD|TEXT FIELD|STRING (for text inputs), AFCOMPONENT|FIELD|NUMBER FIELD|NUMBER (for number inputs), etc.

is clicked.description: TERMINAL NODE: Event trigger operator for button click. Used in TRIGGER_EVENT to specify that the rule triggers when a button is clicked.

is initialized.description: TERMINAL NODE: Event trigger operator for component initialization. Used in TRIGGER_EVENT to specify that the rule triggers when a component is initialized/loaded.

is changed.description: TERMINAL NODE: Event trigger operator for value change. Used in TRIGGER_EVENT to specify that the rule triggers when a field's value changes.
is changed.applies_to: Fields with values (text input, number, date, checkbox, file attachment)
